name: TAO Failed Breakdown Hold v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing a failed breakdown reclaim that must hold before continuation entry. The edge should come from downside rejection plus value hold, not from blindly fading weakness.
invalidation_condition: Abort if failed-breakdown reclaims remain too sparse, if hold confirmation does not improve continuation quality, or if the bounce dies before covering costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks below short-term support, quickly reclaims value, and only becomes tradable if that reclaimed level actually holds instead of failing again.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In choppy conditions, breakdowns often fail. Reclaim-plus-hold logic should capture the better repairs while avoiding blind bottom-picking.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is useful because failed breakdowns and sharp repairs are more visible on a volatile asset, giving this mechanism a fair density test.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If failed breakdowns only mean-revert weakly and do not continue, the hold-confirmed entry may still be late.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries engage but continuation is too brief, reduce target ambition before changing the reclaim logic.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; breakdown and reclaim-hold are sequential and broad
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad reclaim-hold logic instead of exact candle choreography

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.15 * ATR_14 by selling 20%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.15 * ATR_14 by covering 20%
